summary: When a test fails after your change, verify the test's own assumptions before fixing your code
problem: Test fails after a refactor and you immediately assume your change broke something, leading to reverting correct code
solution: Check whether the test was testing implementation details rather than behavior - if the test assumes a specific internal structure that you legitimately changed, the test needs updating, not your code
root_cause: Tests written against implementation details become brittle; a failing test after a refactor is not always a regression
